name: hybrid_prism_gmsh_openfoam_v0_1
description: Default non-scoring OpenFOAM plumbing mesh backend: custom direct body-fitted prism shell near the aircraft, Gmsh tetrahedral external farfield volume, and exact OpenFOAM polyMesh interface merge.

validated_scope.geometry_set: approved full-path solid faired-cap inlet variants fcv01 through fcv05

source_surface.raw_stl_note: Raw exporter STLs are not yet assumed to be direct inputs to this backend; automatic conditioning/gating remains required before optimizer production use.

openfoam_merge.method: exact interface face merge after gmshToFoam conversion

solver_smoke.not_scoring_reason: potentialFoam is inviscid plumbing evidence and does not provide trusted drag/lift

steady_solver_development.openfoam13_note: OpenFOAM 13 uses the modular foamRun incompressibleFluid solver path for this steady incompressible setup; this is the current simpleFoam-style development path.

force_coeffs.reference_policy: development placeholder only until aerodynamic reference area and moment center are formalized

latest_probe.failure_summary: laminar no-slip steady solve diverged by time step 2 and hit pressure-solver floating point exception

known_limitations: This backend is proven on conditioned watertight surfaces, not arbitrary raw exporter STLs.
The farfield policy is currently fixed-padding in the evidence run; dynamic optimizer-scale farfield sizing still needs to be ported into this hybrid driver.
The steady no-slip OpenFOAM probe fails quickly and is not suitable for scoring.
The current three prism layers are a near-wall development shell, not a validated y-plus/wall-treatment strategy.
No force or moment coefficient from this backend is scoring-valid yet.
next_required_work: Add raw-exporter surface conditioning/gating as an explicit upstream step before hybrid meshing.
Port dynamic farfield sizing into the hybrid driver.
Localize the pressure-solver failure in the no-slip steady probe and test whether the issue is wall treatment, single farfield boundary policy, prism transition, or solver setup.
Define reference area, reference length, and moment center from candidate geometry before any force coefficient is used by the optimizer.
